Remember your vision should be on your smartphone. Keep looking at it every day. Every time you open your phone, look at one part of it. Make sure that vision is getting to you viscerally and make sure it has your wife. All I want to say right now is remember you got to keep looking at it. It was the top of government coverage. But it really has to do with a concept that explains why looping is so important. I think this will really help you, almost remind you as you're going through your presentations and your loops, that you're doing things for a reason and it makes perfect sense. So essentially by me saying this to you, I'm hoping that it starts to give context and meaning to what you're doing. I use the straight line system because it works. And that's a good enough reason. I always say how does AA work? How does AA work? How does alcohol work? It works really well. How does it work? Who gives a s***? It just works. In other words, we go to a light bulb and we go to a light switch. We turn it on and we just know the light's coming on. We don't have to understand how every step in the process works. Magnetism and electricity and the wiring. You say, well, that's good enough for me if I can put the light switch on and it works. I'm happy to use lights. I don't have to learn everything about it. Right? Turning it. Beautiful day by the way. It was almost calm enough to walk right on the water and not have the waves destroy the sound. Maybe tomorrow the waves will come down and walk on the water. Anyway. So anyway, so as I was saying, you don't have to understand how everything works to use something, how each step works. But I think this will really help you in the sense that it has to with a lot about why I'm asking you to put your vision on your phone screen. Why I make you resell each of the three tens as you loop. In other words, you don't just when you loop what you don't do is you don't button up your first 10 about the product and then don't talk about it anymore. Every time you run a loop, you always go back and sort of button up whatever you've done before you loop back, you reinforce the last point and then you move forward. So in other words, you might have created an incredibly tight airtight logical case. But even though you've created it and the guys are going, Oh yeah, I love the, oh my God, it's great. Yeah, it makes sense to me. Right. When you do your next loop, if he doesn't say yes at the end of this loop, you're still going to button up that case. You're going to give another point, another reminder about why that case really that you made is really is that logical why you should be in 10. And the reason for that is I'm getting to this point which cuts to everything we do is this idea that certainly wanes. And you've heard me say this before, but I was going through some old video clips last night. So my trains and I realized something very interesting that it's not just certainty that wanes basically everything wanes, whatever human beings do, whatever we get to a point that whether it's absolute certainty, absolute clarity, absolute confidence, absolute understanding, absolute compassion, whatever it is. And yet five minutes later, we're not a certain we don't understand quite as clearly. We're not as confident. We're not as compassionate. In other words, that ultra high level of certainty or clarity or focus that we can get to in a single moment in time. It doesn't last very long. It wanes. It starts to drop until something becomes a conviction and that takes time. That takes repetition. It takes, you know, blocking out competing messages. It just takes time for anything. There's a conviction and that's very difficult to create in someone. I did it with strand over time through repetitive messaging and then showing them proof and again and again. Right. But remember that when you're selling certainty, wanes, certainty, your product, certainty about you and certainty about your company. What do I mean? Again, I repeat this is that they you might have given an incredibly tight. Let me go back. Let's say you give a great presentation, right. And you've framed. I'm going to make this real. You framed a really powerful airtight logical case, right. You framed it. They say, let me think about it. You do your first loop, you go back down and you fill in the blanks, actually take, pick up where that airtight logical frame left off. And now you fill in the blanks and now you make it into this sort of super airtight scream from the hilltops airtight logical case where they say, wow, this really makes it. It really, it really is. You got them to that 10 on the certainty skills airtight. But here's the deal. By the time you finish that entire loop, because that's the first thing you'll do is is reinforce the airtight logical case for the product. Three minutes later, they've almost thought they've already started to like, they're certainly starts to wane and drop slightly. So very often, even by the time you ask for the order, they may have dropped from an absolute 10 to a 9.8.
